content.problemStatement:
Kulyash believes in equality.

Given an integer NNN, output a binary string of length NNN such that:


The count of 010101 subsequences in the string is **equal** to the count of 101010 subsequences;
The string has **at least** one occurrence of 000 as well as 111.
If multiple such strings exist, print any. Also, it is guaranteed that corresponding to the given input, an answer always exists.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains of a single line of input, an integer NNN - the length of the binary string.


Output Format
For each test case, output any binary string of length NNN satisfying the given conditions.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: A binary string satisfying both the conditions is 100110011001. The count of 010101 as well as 101010 subsequences in the string is 222.
Test case 222: A binary string satisfying both the conditions is 010010010. The count of 010101 as well as 101010 subsequences in the string is 111.
